items.title: Gradient-based training
items.description: Each ∂L/∂θᵢ tells an optimizer how the loss responds to a small change in parameter θᵢ; collecting all partials enables gradient descent and Adam-style updates in neural networks and LLMs.

items.title: Backpropagation through layers
items.description: Automatic differentiation computes partial derivatives of the loss with respect to every weight and activation, propagating sensitivities backward through composed functions.

items.title: Input attribution and interpretability
items.description: Partials ∂output/∂featureᵢ quantify how sensitive a model prediction is to each input feature at a given point—used in saliency maps and local explanation methods.

items.title: Multivariate optimization and loss landscapes
items.description: Partial derivatives describe slope along each parameter axis on a loss surface, guiding learning-rate schedules, saddle-point analysis and convergence diagnostics.
